projectDescription: ESPHome is a firmware framework for ESP32, ESP8266, BK72xx, and RP2040 microcontrollers that lets you configure devices using simple YAML files and integrate them with home automation systems like Home Assistant.
keyFeatures: YAML-based device configuration — no coding required for most use cases
Support for ESP32, ESP8266, BK72xx (LibreTiny), RP2040, and Zephyr platforms
700+ built-in component integrations (sensors, lights, switches, climate, displays, and more)
Native Home Assistant integration via encrypted API protocol
MQTT support with auto-discovery for any home automation hub
Automation engine with triggers, conditions, and actions
Substitutions and packages for reusable, modular configuration
Web dashboard for managing multiple devices
Bluetooth proxy and BLE tracker support
Voice assistant integration for local speech processing
publicApiSurface: esphome upload <file.yaml> — flash firmware to device
esphome run <file.yaml> — compile, upload, and show logs
esphome update-all — OTA update all devices
esphome idedata <file.yaml> — print IDE build data
esphome analyze-memory <file.yaml> — analyze firmware memory usage
esphome.codegen — Python API for custom component code generation
esphome.config_validation (cv) — schema validators for component configuration
IS_PLATFORM_COMPONENT, CODEOWNERS, AUTO_LOAD, DEPENDENCIES — component metadata

Component YAML keys: esphome, wifi, mqtt, api, ota, logger, sensor, binary_sensor, switch, light, climate, cover, fan, button, number, select, text_sensor, display, uart, i2c, spi, script, substitutions, packages, external_components, web_server, bluetooth_proxy, voice_assistant
